one_liner: TSLA -7.49% to $393 on 1.56x volume — biggest single-day drop in nearly a year despite Q2 deliveries beating by 74K units; textbook sell-the-news at 421x P/E with inventory drawdown concerns.

price_action.vol_rank: Elevated — 1.56x, top-5 session in recent weeks
price_action.intraday_story: TSLA tagged $432.35 at the open then collapsed continuously to $389.30, closing at $393.45 near the lows. The $42 high-to-low range on 1.56x volume is a definitive distribution event. Sellers who bought the delivery beat news unloaded into early retail buyers. Close in bottom 10% of range on above-average volume is the most bearish possible candle structure.

news_catalyst.headline: Q2 deliveries 480K beat estimates by 74K — stock fell 7.5% anyway on sell-the-news, inventory drawdown, and valuation concerns
news_catalyst.summary: Tesla delivered 480,126 vehicles in Q2 2026, +25% YoY, beating the 406K consensus by 74K — one of the largest beats in company history. Yet the stock had its worst day in nearly a year. The stock had already rallied 12% in prior weeks pricing in the beat. More damaging: production came in at 451,758 units — 28K below deliveries — meaning Tesla drew down inventory rather than building buffer. EV tax credit expiration has Cox Automotive projecting a 20% US sales drop. At 421x P/E there is no valuation support.

technical.summary: Today is a textbook Upthrust After Distribution (UTAD): TSLA briefly cleared $432 — new recent highs — only to reverse savagely and close near the day lows on 1.56x volume. This is not a shakeout; it is genuine supply. The $389.30 intraday low is now critical support. Stocks with Josh documented that after a 10%+ flush on good news (not bad news), Tesla is historically higher 30 days later 85% of the time — but only if $369 holds. Below $369 opens $338. DCF at $56.70 base vs $393 price means zero fundamental floor. Volume on a genuine capitulation flush would be 2x+ with a sharp intraday reversal — today closed near the lows, suggesting sellers remain in control going into next week.

wyckoff_narrative: The failed breakout to $432 followed by a close near the day low on 1.56x volume reads as an Upthrust (UT) — a Wyckoff signal that precedes a larger markdown. The prior July 1 down move and today sequence constitutes overhead supply announcement. For accumulation thesis to reassert: TSLA must hold $369 on low volume then stage a high-volume bounce back above $393-400. Until that sequence, path of least resistance is lower.

valuation.summary: DCF base $56.70 vs $393 = -594% margin of safety. Trading at 7x fundamental value. Entire premium reflects FSD/Robotaxi/Optimus optionality. No fundamental floor.

key_strengths: Q2 deliveries beat by 74K units — demand real enough to achieve
key_concerns: Production below deliveries: drew down inventory to hit headline
EV tax credit expiration; Cox projects -20% US sales H2 2026
fundamentals.summary: Tesla delivery beat quality undermined by inventory drawdown. Valuation at 421x provides no buffer against disappointment.

risks: Close near the low on 1.56x volume — distribution signal; pressure may continue into next week
$369 prior pivot low is critical; violation opens $338
EV tax credit expiration is unquantified demand headwind in H2
Production below deliveries raises Q3 inventory build questions
